_comment: Single source of truth for the external dataroom tools. Every tool is a thin wrapper over the embedding (jina-embeddings-v5-text-small) or reranking (jina-reranker-v3) backend in server/dataroom_service.py - same two models, different interface shapes/names/docstrings. The extension (pi/extensions/dataroom-search.ts) registers each; server/app.py validates the UI selection against this list and serves it to the homepage. param types: s=string, s[]=string[], i?=optional int, n?=optional number, f?=optional float. 'op' is the backend endpoint. 'fixed' merges constant body fields. 'rename' maps an arg name to a different body key.

tools.name: sentence_embed
tools.desc: Embed text string(s) with jina-embeddings-v5-text-small and APPEND the vectors to a jsonl file in your working directory. Returns only {path,count,dim} - NOT the vectors (read them back from the file yourself). You decide what to embed and how to use the vectors. L2-normalized (dot == cosine). INPUT: {texts: string[], role?: "query"|"passage" (default passage), out?: filename (default embeddings.jsonl)}. FILE: one json/line {i,text,role,dim,embedding}.

tools.name: embed_query
tools.desc: Embed a single query string (query role) with jina-embeddings-v5-text-small, appending the vector to a jsonl file. Returns {path,count,dim}. Convenience form of sentence_embed for the question side.

tools.name: embed_passages
tools.desc: Embed one or more passages (passage role) with jina-embeddings-v5-text-small, appending vectors to a jsonl file. Returns {path,count,dim}. Convenience form of sentence_embed for the document side.

tools.name: semantic_search
tools.desc: Find passages in the dataroom by meaning (embedding similarity), embedding on the fly over the chosen scope. INPUT: {query, k?, paths?, chunk_size?}. Returns top-k chunks as {path,chunk,score,text}.

tools.name: vector_search
tools.desc: Dense retrieval over the dataroom: embeds your query and the corpus, returns the k nearest chunks by cosine similarity. INPUT: {query, k?, paths?}. Returns {path,chunk,score,text}.

tools.name: find_in_dataroom
tools.desc: Look something up in the dataroom by meaning rather than exact keywords. INPUT: {query, k?}. Returns the most relevant chunks with their source file and a relevance score.

tools.name: retrieve_context
tools.desc: Retrieve the top supporting passages for a question from the dataroom (RAG-style retrieval). INPUT: {question, k?}. Returns ranked chunks {path,chunk,score,text}.

tools.name: lookup_evidence
tools.desc: Find evidence in the dataroom that supports or refutes a claim, by semantic match. INPUT: {claim, k?}. Returns relevant passages with sources and scores.

tools.name: passage_rerank
tools.desc: Score caller-supplied passages by relevance to a query with jina-reranker-v3 (cross-encoder). You supply the candidates. INPUT: {query, documents: string[], top_n?}. OUTPUT: {results:[{index,score,text}]} sorted by score desc.

tools.name: sort_by_relevance
tools.desc: Rerank a list of documents by how well each matches a query, using the Jina reranker. INPUT: {query, documents: string[], top_n?}. Returns documents sorted by relevance with scores.

tools.name: rank_documents
tools.desc: Order a set of documents from most to least relevant to a topic (cross-encoder reranking). INPUT: {topic, documents: string[]}. Returns ranked {index,score,text}.

tools.name: pick_best_passage
tools.desc: Return the single passage most relevant to a query (reranker, top-1). INPUT: {query, documents: string[]}. Returns the best {index,score,text}.

tools.name: top3_passages
tools.desc: Return the 3 passages most relevant to a query (reranker, top-3). INPUT: {query, documents: string[]}. Returns the best three {index,score,text}.

tools.name: cosine_similarity
tools.desc: Compute pairwise cosine similarity between two equal-length lists of texts (embedding dot product, normalized). INPUT: {a: string[], b: string[]}. Returns {similarities: number[]}.

tools.name: similarity_matrix
tools.desc: Compute the full cosine-similarity matrix between queries and documents via embeddings. INPUT: {queries: string[], documents: string[]}. Returns {matrix: number[][]} (queries x documents).

tools.name: nearest_texts
tools.desc: Given one query and many candidate texts, score each candidate by embedding cosine similarity to the query. INPUT: {query, candidates: string[]}. Returns a similarity matrix (1 x N) you can argsort.

tools.name: classify_text
tools.desc: Zero-shot classify texts into user-defined labels using embeddings (each text -> nearest label). INPUT: {texts: string[], labels: string[]}. Returns {results:[{text,label,score}]}.

tools.name: zero_shot_label
tools.desc: Assign the best-matching label to a single text from candidate labels, via embedding similarity. INPUT: {text, labels: string[]}. Returns {results:[{text,label,score}]}.

tools.name: deduplicate_texts
tools.desc: Select the top-k semantically DIVERSE strings (submodular/greedy over embeddings) - i.e. drop near-duplicates, keep a representative spread. INPUT: {strings: string[], k?}. Returns {kept_indices, kept}.

tools.name: select_diverse
tools.desc: Pick a diverse subset of items that best covers the semantic space (embedding facility-location). INPUT: {items: string[], k?}. Returns {kept_indices, kept}.

tools.name: cluster_texts
tools.desc: Group texts into clusters of near-duplicates/related items by embedding similarity (greedy threshold). INPUT: {texts: string[], threshold?} (default 0.82). Returns {n_clusters, clusters:[{id,members,texts}]}.

tools.name: group_similar
tools.desc: Cluster a list of items so semantically similar ones are grouped together (embedding similarity). INPUT: {items: string[], threshold?}. Returns clusters of items.
